name: Carioca Portuguese
notes: Carioca (Rio de Janeiro city). Hallmark: coda /S/ palatalised to [ʃ]/[ʒ] (the 'chiado carioca') - modelled as post-lexical allophone_rules (RJ_CHIADO_CODA_S/Z), reaching 91% word-internally and 76% word-finally in cultured Rio speech (Callou 2010:135), the defining feature and unique among the major Brazilian capitals (mesmo -> [ˈmeʒmu], paz -> [ˈpaʃ], dois -> [ˈdojʃ]). Strong dorsal onset /r/ -> [χ~x]; non-final coda /R/ -> velar fricative [x], word-final coda /R/ -> aspiration [h] or deletion (Callou 2010:138). Categorical /t,d/ palatalisation. One of the two media-standard accents of Brazil.

sources.author: Callou, D. & Leite, Y.
sources.title: Iniciação à fonética e à fonologia (8th ed.)

sources.author: Cardoso, S.A.M. et al.
sources.title: Atlas Linguístico do Brasil (ALiB), vols. 1-2
sources.notes: Geolinguistic atlas; pretonic-vowel and palatalization isoglosses.

sources.author: Callou, D. (with Leite, Y. & Moraes, J.)
sources.title: Um perfil da fala carioca. In: Ribeiro, S.S.C., Costa, S.B.B. & Cardoso, S.A.M. (orgs.), Dos sons às palavras: nas trilhas da língua portuguesa
sources.notes: NURC cultured-speech corpus (5 capitals). p.135, Tabela 1: coda /S/ palatalisation ('chiado carioca') in Rio reaches 91% word-internally and 76% word-finally (1990s real-time data), vs alveolar 4%/19% - the defining Carioca feature, highest among the major capitals. p.137-138: coda /R/ posteriorised; Callou (1987) - the predominant cultured-Carioca coda /R/ is the velar fricative [x], except word-finally where deletion or aspiration [h] prevail. Analyses co-authored with Leite & Moraes (1996, 2002, 2003).

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).

allophone_rules.notes: The 'chiado carioca': coda /S/ palatalises to [ʃ] word-finally and pre-consonantally (mesmo, paz, dois). Callou 2010:135 (Tabela 1) - 91% word-internal, 76% word-final in cultured Rio speech; the defining Carioca feature, unique among the major Brazilian capitals. Post-lexical layer, mirroring pt-PT PT_CODA_S_HUSH; the pt-BR base keeps an alveolar coda /s/, which this delta overrides.
Voiced counterpart of RJ_CHIADO_CODA_S: a coda /z/ surfaces as [ʒ] before a voiced consonant (Callou 2010:135). Word-final coda sibilants devoice to [ʃ] via the base [s] value.
Word-final coda /R/: aspiration [h] (or deletion) prevails in cultured Carioca speech (Callou 2010:138, citing Callou 1987). Modelled as aspiration rather than deletion so the segment is retained.

Non-final coda /R/ (pre-consonantal: porta, carta) is the velar fricative [x], the predominant cultured-Carioca variant (Callou 2010:138, Callou 1987). Onset and intervocalic rhotics are untouched (they are not in the coda).

location.notes: Rio de Janeiro city — the Carioca variety. [Wikidata Q8678 P625]
